quick_facts.label: Recruiting channel
quick_facts.value: ECG Technician is recruited as a Railway Recruitment Board (RRB) paramedical category when it is included in a Centralised Employment Notice (CEN), rather than through the general railway technician stream.

quick_facts.label: Qualification route in the most recent cited CEN
quick_facts.value: CEN 03/2025 required 10+2 or graduation in Science plus a certificate, diploma or degree in ECG Laboratory Technology, Cardiology, Cardiology Technician or Cardiology Techniques from a reputed institution. A certificate had to be at least one year long.

quick_facts.label: Recruitment parameters in CEN 03/2025
quick_facts.value: The August 2025 notice listed ECG Technician at 7th CPC Pay Level 4, initial basic pay of ₹25,500, C1 medical standard, and age 18–33 as on January 1, 2026. These were notice-specific parameters, not a guarantee for future CENs.

quick_facts.label: Selection method in CEN 03/2025
quick_facts.value: The notice prescribed a single-stage computer-based test (CBT), followed by document verification and the prescribed railway medical examination; final empanelment depended on merit, document verification and medical fitness.

quick_facts.label: Latest cited vacancy context
quick_facts.value: CEN 03/2025 advertised four ECG Technician vacancies across all participating RRBs. Railway vacancies are expressly provisional and can change by notification, so this should not be treated as an ongoing vacancy count.

sections.heading: What the role involves
paragraphs: The RRB ECG Technician syllabus covers anatomy, physiology, biochemistry, pharmacology, cardiovascular disease, and basic and advanced cardiovascular investigations. This indicates a cardiac-diagnostics support role rather than an independent clinician role.

The Healthcare Sector Skill Council’s occupational standard for the closely aligned Cardiac Care Technician role gives concrete task examples: prepare the ECG area, attach and operate electrodes and leads, explain the procedure, monitor the patient for abnormalities, maintain equipment and supplies, and forward technically prepared results to the attending physician for interpretation. The standard describes support to the provider; it does not assign clinical diagnosis to the technician.
bullets: Prepare the patient, room and ECG equipment according to local procedure.
Obtain technically accurate ECG tracings through correct electrode/lead placement and equipment operation.
Recognize and promptly report abnormal observations to the responsible clinician; preserve the clinician’s role in interpretation and treatment decisions.
Maintain records, equipment, consumables and infection-control/workplace procedures required by the employing railway medical unit.

sections.heading: Entry route through RRB paramedical recruitment
paragraphs: For the most recent cited recruitment, the practical entry sequence was: obtain the prescribed science education and ECG/cardiology qualification; apply against an ECG Technician vacancy in the relevant RRB paramedical CEN; clear the CBT; then pass document verification and the railway medical examination. Candidates must hold the required qualification by the application closing date; candidates awaiting final results were ineligible under CEN 03/2025.

The CEN 03/2025 CBT was 90 minutes and 100 objective questions. It allocated 70 questions to professional ability and 30 to general awareness, arithmetic/intelligence/reasoning, and general science combined; one-third of the marks for a question were deducted for a wrong answer. For ECG Technician, the professional syllabus specifically included cardiovascular investigations.
bullets: Build eligibility around the exact qualification title, duration and recognition language in the live CEN; do not assume that a similarly named private course will be accepted.
Use official RRB sites for notices, applications, corrigenda, exam information and results; RRB Chandigarh explicitly warns candidates against relying on social-media notices.
Treat age limits, vacancies, eligibility details, medical standards and dates as recruitment-cycle-specific.

sections.heading: Skills to develop
paragraphs: Technical preparation should cover the RRB professional syllabus: human sciences underpinning ECG work, pharmacology relevant to cardiac care, cardiovascular disease, and basic and advanced cardiovascular investigations. This is also the most direct guide to CBT preparation for the ECG Technician category.

In practice, the aligned national occupational standard emphasizes patient communication, correct electrode placement, calibration and artifact-free tracings, observation during procedures, documentation, escalation of abnormal findings, and equipment/supply upkeep. These are useful employability skills alongside formal eligibility.
bullets: Cardiac anatomy, physiology and common cardiovascular conditions.
ECG lead/electrode placement, tracing quality checks and basic equipment handling.
Patient identification, clear procedure explanation, privacy, reassurance and respectful communication.
Accurate record handling, technical reporting to the clinician and prompt escalation within protocol.
Safety, cleanliness, equipment care and adherence to the employing facility’s procedures.

sections.heading: Railway employment setting
paragraphs: An appointed candidate is allotted to a railway zone or production unit based on merit, preferences, medical standard and vacancy position. The CEN states that a railway servant is ordinarily employed on the railway or establishment of first appointment, while the competent authority may transfer the employee according to service needs.

Railway records also place ECG Technicians within the Medical Department alongside laboratory, radiography, cardiology, physiotherapy and other allied-health categories. Accordingly, the role should be understood as a railway medical-services post, with the exact unit and location determined by the recruitment allotment and administration.
bullets: Posting location and unit are not fully controllable: candidates select options, but allotment remains subject to merit, eligibility and vacancy availability.

Newly appointed staff should not assume an immediate inter-railway transfer; CEN 03/2025 stated that requests would generally not be registered until at least five to ten years of service had been completed.
Clinical routines, roster arrangements and the precise range of procedures can vary by railway medical establishment and local service requirements.

sections.heading: Pay context
paragraphs: For dated context only, CEN 03/2025 listed ECG Technician at 7th CPC Pay Level 4 with an initial basic pay of ₹25,500. This is basic pay stated in that recruitment notice, not an estimate of take-home pay.

Actual monthly take-home pay can differ because it depends on applicable allowances, deductions, place of posting and rules in force at appointment. Future RRB notifications may also revise or state parameters differently, so applicants should verify the live CEN before applying.
bullets: Use the pay level and initial basic pay in the specific CEN as the authoritative recruitment reference.
Do not equate the stated initial basic pay with total gross salary or in-hand salary.
Vacancy counts and recruitment parameters in a CEN are provisional and may be increased, reduced or cancelled by the railway administration.
